title: Weather warning: Yellow level 2 warning for damaging waves
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The South African...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The South African Weather Service has issued a Yellow Level 2 warning for damaging waves along parts of the Western Cape and Namaqua coastline, valid from 01:00 on Sunday, 1 June until 12:59 on Monday, 2 June.


> Forecast Conditions:


Heavy westerly to south-westerly swells with wave heights of 4.0 to 4.5 meters are expected between Saldanha Bay and Plettenberg Bay on Sunday, extending from Cape Point to Plettenberg Bay on Monday.


> Potential Impacts:


- Disruption of small harbours and ports.
- Possible damage to coastal infrastructure.
- Dangerous sea conditions for vessels.
- Disruption to beachfront activities.
- Risk of the public being swept off rocks due to large waves.


> Public Advisory:


Beachgoers, fishermen, and coastal communities are advised to exercise extreme caution, avoid rocky coastal areas, and monitor updates from SAWS.

For more information, visit: www.weathersa.co.za

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
